chapter: Chapter: THE WORLD OF METALS AND NON-METALS

questions.question: Why are copper wires used in household electrical wiring instead of iron?
questions.answer: Copper is a much better conductor of electricity than iron, which reduces energy loss as heat.

questions.question: What property of metals allows them to be hammered into thin sheets?
questions.answer: Malleability

questions.question: Name a non-metal that is a good conductor of electricity.
questions.answer: Graphite (a form of Carbon)

questions.question: Why do bells in temples make a ringing sound when struck?
questions.answer: Metals are sonorous, meaning they produce a deep, ringing sound when hit with a hard object.

questions.question: Explain why sodium is stored in kerosene.
questions.answer: Sodium is highly reactive and reacts vigorously with oxygen and moisture in the air, potentially catching fire.

questions.question: Which non-metal is essential for our life and is inhaled during breathing?
questions.answer: Oxygen

questions.question: What is the nature of the oxide formed when magnesium burns in air?
questions.answer: Basic nature (Magnesium Oxide)

questions.question: Why are aluminum foils used to wrap food items?
questions.answer: Because aluminum is highly malleable and does not react with food under normal conditions.

questions.question: Name the gas produced when metals react with dilute acids.
questions.answer: Hydrogen gas

questions.question: What is the 'Pop sound' test used for in the laboratory?
questions.answer: It is used to confirm the presence of Hydrogen gas.

questions.question: Why are handles of cooking utensils made of plastic or wood?
questions.answer: Plastic and wood are poor conductors of heat (insulators), preventing our hands from getting burnt.

questions.question: Which metal exists in liquid state at room temperature?
questions.answer: Mercury

questions.question: State the difference between metals and non-metals based on ductility.
questions.answer: Metals are ductile (can be drawn into wires), while non-metals are non-ductile and brittle.

questions.question: What happens when sulphur dioxide reacts with water?
questions.answer: It forms Sulphurous acid ($H_2SO_3$), which is acidic in nature.

questions.question: Why can copper not displace zinc from its salt solution?
questions.answer: Because copper is less reactive than zinc.

questions.question: Name a non-metal used in water purification to kill germs.
questions.answer: Chlorine

questions.question: What is an alloy of iron, nickel, and chromium called?
questions.answer: Stainless Steel

questions.question: Why is phosphorus stored in water?
questions.answer: Phosphorus is a very reactive non-metal that catches fire if exposed to air.

questions.question: Which metal is used in thermometers?
questions.answer: Mercury

questions.question: Define 'Lustre' and name a lustrous non-metal.
questions.answer: Lustre is the shiny appearance of a surface; Iodine is a lustrous non-metal.

questions.question: What is the general nature of non-metal oxides?
questions.answer: Acidic or Neutral

questions.question: Name two metals that are soft enough to be cut with a knife.
questions.answer: Sodium and Potassium

questions.question: What happens when an iron nail is placed in a copper sulphate solution?
questions.answer: Iron displaces copper, forming green iron sulphate and a brown copper coating on the nail.

questions.question: Which non-metal is used in purple-colored antiseptic solutions applied on wounds?
questions.answer: Iodine

questions.question: Why do metals generally have high melting points?
questions.answer: Because of the strong forces of attraction between the atoms in a metal lattice.
